ticket.title: Multi-worktree sync: worktree-aware claim + CRDT .hack/ + rebase-ids
handoff: Design doc at architecture/multi-worktree-sync.md. P2 LANDED (see R002-T1 in hack-board/src/server.ts): per-relay shards at .hack/events/<id>.jsonl, 'scan' event type keyed on content hash, legacy-log migration on first serve. diffTicket / diffAndLog / snapshot / replaySnapshot removed from server.ts; scan_disappeared in status.rs now walks shards with legacy fallback. P1 SCOPE GENERALIZED — see updated @hack:next(P1): instead of just `git worktree list`, use a gitignored `.hack/worktrees.json` registry that can declare local clones, git-worktree subtrees, and SSH-remote peers; `git worktree list` becomes one auto-discovery source, not the whole story. Phases remaining: P1 (sibling registry + claim), P3 (per-todo files), P4 (scoped merge driver), P5 (rebase-ids).

P1 LANDED. New rs-hack/src/worktrees.rs holds the .hack/worktrees.json registry (Sibling::{Local,Git,Remote}), git worktree list --porcelain auto-discovery, and the sibling-ID union scan (subprocess for local/git, ssh for remote). New 'rs-hack board worktrees add|list|remove' subcommands manage the file. handle_claim now unions board.tickets ∪ union_sibling_ids before picking max(id)+1, so all three ID paths (compound -Tn, standalone, bare relay) are sibling-aware. Per-sibling failures emit a stderr warning and proceed (race resolution stays P5's job). .hack/worktrees.json added to .gitignore. Smoke verified: two local siblings allocate R003/R004 not R003/R003; unreachable ssh peer warns and still allocates.
next_steps: P1: Sibling workspace registry for `board claim` anti-collision. Add gitignored `.hack/worktrees.json` with `{siblings: [{kind: local|git|remote, path, host?}]}`. On claim: union IDs across self + every sibling (local = subprocess, git = auto-discovered from `git worktree list --porcelain` merged with the JSON, remote = `ssh <host> rs-hack board tickets -f json -p <path>`). Compound sub-ticket scans (R007-Tn) query siblings the same way. Warn-per-site-fail and proceed; race-resolution is P5's job. New subcommand surface: `rs-hack board worktrees add|list|remove`. Original @hack:next: 'Walk siblings via git worktree list --porcelain, union IDs, take shared lock at <git-common-dir>/hack-id.lock.' — that remains the implementation for the git-subtree kind; other kinds are new.
P3: Per-todo files .hack/todos/<id>.md. Dir instead of single file. Update both parse_todos in status.rs and parseTodos in server.ts. One-shot migration on first serve/status.

P4: Scoped merge driver for _todos.jsonl / _renamed.jsonl. Hidden `rs-hack board _merge-events %O %A %B` subcommand (concat + sort by t,id,type + dedupe). .gitattributes + `git config merge.hack-events.driver` installed by `board init`. Per-relay shards do not depend on it.

P5: `rs-hack board rebase-ids`. Detect duplicate @hack:ticket/@hack:relay IDs after cross-machine merge. Pick winner by earliest `git log --follow --diff-filter=A` timestamp on the annotation line. Rename loser via existing AST machinery, plus scoped string-replacement inside @hack: citation strings. Shard-rename the ticket's events file (OLD.jsonl → NEW.jsonl via `git mv`). Append to _renamed.jsonl. Dry-run by default.
P3: Per-todo files .hack/todos/<id>.md. Dir instead of single file. Update both parse_todos in status.rs and parseTodos in server.ts. One-shot migration on first serve/status.

P4: Scoped merge driver for _todos.jsonl / _renamed.jsonl. Hidden 'rs-hack board _merge-events %O %A %B' subcommand (concat + sort by t,id,type + dedupe). .gitattributes + 'git config merge.hack-events.driver' installed by 'board init'. Per-relay shards do not depend on it.

P5: 'rs-hack board rebase-ids'. Detect duplicate @hack:ticket/@hack:relay IDs after cross-machine merge. Pick winner by earliest 'git log --follow --diff-filter=A' timestamp on the annotation line. Rename loser via existing AST machinery, plus scoped string-replacement inside @hack: citation strings. Shard-rename the ticket's events file (OLD.jsonl → NEW.jsonl via 'git mv'). Append to _renamed.jsonl. Dry-run by default.
cleanup: Consider @hack: citation sigil (e.g. {R008}) for unambiguous cross-reference — deferred; not required for phase 1.
verify: Smoke: two local siblings registered in .hack/worktrees.json; `board claim --kind relay` in each produces non-colliding R-numbers.
Smoke: remote sibling declared via ssh; `board claim` logs a warning when the host is unreachable and allocates anyway.
gotchas: The ssh-shell-quote in worktrees.rs is intentionally minimal — paths with shell metachars get single-quoted. If a remote path has unusual chars, double-check the produced ssh command.
